title: dalbergia berteroi
intro: Dalbergia berteroi, also known as the Hawaiian wala'u tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This tree is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bark and roots of the Dalbergia berteroi are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, providing benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from the bark and roots, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
